description: Fix the dev-proxy SIGTERM E2E test that consistently fails in Docker containers. The test checks that worker processes die within 500ms after the proxy receives SIGTERM, but Docker's process termination timing is slower than native macOS, causing the worker PID check to find the process still alive.

userStories.title: Make dev-proxy SIGTERM E2E test resilient to Docker process timing
userStories.description: As a platform contributor running E2E tests in Docker, I want the 'SIGTERM kills worker and proxy exits cleanly' test to pass so that the full E2E suite is green in container environments
acceptanceCriteria: The test at e2e/dev-proxy.e2e.ts:149 passes consistently in Docker containers
The test still verifies that worker processes are cleaned up after SIGTERM
The test does not add excessive wait time (keep total additional wait under 5 seconds)
